editableNewDialogue.text: Random question: in Most Wanted 2005, do pursuits scale more off your Heat level or your career progress?

Mostly Heat level. Heat determines which police units spawn and how aggressive they are (more roadblocks, tougher units, faster escalation). Career progress matters indirectly because you unlock higher Heat caps and end up in longer pursuits more often, but the immediate difficulty jump is usually tied to Heat.
If someone wanted to make pursuits tense without making races miserable, what would you tweak first?

First tweak pursuit tuning rather than global handling: increase police aggression/backup frequency, slightly reduce cooldown spots effectiveness, and make roadblocks more common at higher Heat. Leave opponent rubberbanding and traffic density mostly alone so circuit/sprint races don’t turn into constant RNG.
Is there a downside to turning off autosave when modding?

Biggest downside is losing progress if the game crashes or you forget to save. Upside is you can roll back if a modded unlock, busted bounty value, or corrupted event flag messes with progression. A practical middle ground is manual saves + periodic backups of the save folder.
Unrelated: I read somewhere that early versions of the game had slightly different cop behaviors. Any truth to that?

There were small differences across platforms and patches—spawn timing, pursuit breaker reliability, and some physics quirks. It’s not usually a completely different AI, more like tuning changes. People sometimes notice it because PC with certain fixes/mods can feel harsher than the original console balancing.
One thing I keep doing now that autosave is off is dropping a manual save after every big pursuit, because if the game decides to crash mid-chase I really don’t feel like redoing half the night.

Yeah, with autosave disabled that’s basically your safety net. A “save-after-pursuit” habit makes a lot of sense—especially in MW05 where long chases can eat a ton of time. You could even rotate a couple save slots so one bad save doesn’t wipe you.
About that "next playthrough" thing—I don’t think I’m actually doing the pizza delivery hatchback run. It was just a joke idea I threw out.
Got it—so it’s not a real plan, just a fun hypothetical. In that case, we should treat the pizza hatchback run as a gag concept, not something you’re intending to follow through with.
Yeah, exactly. If I do another run at all, it’ll probably be something else, but the pizza car isn’t on my list.

annotation.queryText: I’m trying to pick a self-imposed rule set for my next game run that matches the kind of weird, ultra-restrictive playthrough I usually come up with. If I wanted the restriction to be centered around using just one very ordinary vehicle for the entire run (to the point it becomes the main challenge), what’s the specific “only-this-vehicle” run idea I’ve mentioned before that would fit that vibe?
